results.rendered_html: vi-˚bhāvanā f. (in rhet.) description of effects the causes of which are left to be conjectured (or accord. to some, ‘description by negatives, bringing out the qualities of any object more clearly than by positive description’),  Vām. ;  Kāvyād.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
vibhāvanaṃ -nā
1 Clear perception or ascertainment, discrimination, judgment.
2 Discussion, investigation, examination.
3 Conception, imagination.
— nā (In Rhet.) A figure of speech in which effects are represented as taking place though their usual causes are absent;
(pb) kriyāyāḥ pratiṣedhepi phalavyaktirvibhāvanā  K. P. 10.
